content: When the 7th lord (spouse, partnership, marriage) occupies the 1st house (self, body, personality), the native's identity and life become strongly oriented toward partnership. Marriage tends to occur relatively early, and the spouse plays an influential, often dominant, role in shaping the native's outlook. Because the 7th lord aspects its own house from the 1st, the marital significations remain well-supported, indicating an engaged and present partner. Parashara associates this placement with a person who values companionship highly and whose personal growth is bound up with married life. The spouse may resemble the native in temperament or come from a familiar background, and the native is naturally drawn toward forming lasting unions.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24 (Bhava Phala); Phaladeepika

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 2nd house (family, accumulated wealth, kutumba, speech), marriage brings expansion of the family and often an increase in wealth after the union. The spouse contributes to family resources, and marital life is closely connected to financial accumulation and domestic life. Parashara indicates gains in wealth through the partner and a marriage that strengthens the household. The spouse may be involved in family enterprise or in managing collective family matters. Speech and the manner of dealing within the family become important to marital harmony, and the union typically supports stability and the growth of the family line.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Saravali

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 3rd house (courage, siblings, communication, short journeys), the spouse is often courageous, communicative, and self-driven, and the marriage may involve travel or relocation. The native takes initiative in seeking partnership, and the union frequently develops through one's own efforts rather than family arrangement. Because the 3rd is a house of effort and desire, the native's marital satisfaction is tied to active engagement and shared enterprise with the partner. Classical texts note the spouse may share interests in communication, media, or skilled work. There can be involvement of siblings or in-laws in the marriage, and the bond strengthens when both partners pursue common goals.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 4th house (home, mother, property, emotional security, happiness), marriage brings domestic comfort, emotional fulfillment, and often property or vehicles through or after the union. The spouse contributes to the happiness of the home and may be closely involved with the native's family and residence. Parashara associates this placement with marital contentment rooted in domestic life and a partner who provides emotional security. The 4th house connection indicates that the native's peace of mind is bound to the quality of married life. Settling down, building a home together, and family-centered living are strong themes, and the marriage tends to anchor the native's emotional world.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Saravali

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 5th house (romance, love, creativity, children, past merit), the marriage often arises from love or strong romantic attraction, and the bond carries warmth and affection. This is a favorable placement for progeny, as the lord of partnership joins the house of children, supporting the birth of offspring through the marriage. Parashara connects this position with happiness in married life and a partner who is loving, creative, or intellectually engaging. The native may meet the spouse through education, creative pursuits, or shared enjoyment. Romance continues to play a role even after marriage, and the union is enriched by affection, children, and the merit of past good deeds.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 6th house (enemies, disease, debt, conflict, service), marital life can face friction, disputes, or health concerns affecting the spouse, since the lord of partnership falls into a dusthana. There may be delays in marriage, disagreements within the union, or interference from third parties, and the native must work consciously to preserve harmony. However, the 6th is an upachaya house, so difficulties often improve over time with sustained effort, and the bond can strengthen after early friction. Parashara cautions about quarrels and obstacles connected to the partner, particularly if the 7th lord is afflicted. A well-dignified 7th lord here can give a hardworking, service-minded spouse and a marriage that endures through challenges.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika Ch.6

content: When the 7th lord occupies its own house (the 7th), the significations of marriage and partnership are strong and well-protected, making this one of the most favorable placements for marital matters. The spouse is prominent, supportive, and central to the native's life, and the partnership tends to be stable and well-formed. Parashara associates the lord in its own bhava with the full and healthy expression of that house's matters, here indicating a fortunate marriage and a capable partner. Business or professional partnerships also prosper, and the native deals well with the public. Marriage typically occurs at the appropriate time, and the union provides a steadying influence, with both partners contributing to a balanced relationship.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Saravali

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 8th house (longevity, transformation, in-laws' wealth, hidden matters, intimacy), marriage is touched by themes of deep transformation and the testing of the bond. There may be delays, sudden changes in marital circumstances, or concern for the spouse's longevity and well-being, since the lord of partnership occupies a house of upheaval. The native may gain wealth through the spouse, inheritance, or in-laws, as the 8th rules others' resources joined to the partnership. Parashara cautions about marital challenges and the need to nurture intimacy and trust. If the 7th lord is strong, this placement can deepen the marital bond through shared crises and confer a partner who transforms the native's life profoundly.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 9th house (fortune, dharma, higher learning, long journeys, father), marriage is fortunate and elevates the native's luck and standing. The spouse is often righteous, well-educated, fortunate, or from a respected background, and the union may come through travel, higher studies, or distant connections. Parashara associates the lord of partnership in the house of fortune with a virtuous and lucky marriage that supports the native's dharma. The spouse may come from a different region, culture, or community. Marriage frequently coincides with a rise in fortune, and the partner acts as a source of good counsel and ethical support, strengthening the native's path in life.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Saravali

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 10th house (career, status, public life, authority), marriage and career become intertwined, and the spouse often supports or advances the native's professional standing. The partner may be ambitious, publicly visible, or directly involved in the native's work, and marriage frequently brings a rise in social status. Parashara connects this placement with prominence achieved through partnership and a spouse who plays a public role. Business alliances and professional partnerships also flourish. The native may meet the spouse through work, and the marriage tends to enhance reputation and worldly success, though the demands of career can require conscious balancing to protect marital harmony.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 11th house (gains, fulfillment of desires, large networks, friends, income), marriage fulfills the native's desires and brings gains, often including financial benefit through the spouse. The 11th is a strongly auspicious house, so the lord of partnership here indicates a satisfying union that helps realize the native's aspirations. The spouse may come through friends, social circles, or networks, and the partner often expands the native's social and financial standing. Parashara associates this placement with profit and the gratification of wishes through marriage. The union supports the native's goals, and married life tends to be prosperous, socially active, and aligned with the fulfillment of long-held desires.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Saravali

content: When the 7th lord occupies the 12th house (loss, expenditure, foreign lands, isolation, bed pleasures, moksha), marriage may involve separation, distance, foreign connection, or significant expenditure related to the partner. The spouse could be from a distant place or different background, or the native may settle abroad after marriage. Parashara cautions about possible separation, time spent apart, or the dilution of marital significations, since the lord of partnership occupies a house of loss. Yet the 12th also rules bed comforts and private intimacy, so the conjugal bond can be tender in seclusion. If the 7th lord is strong, this placement can support a spiritually inclined partner and a marriage that grows through periods of distance or surrender.
source: Brihat Parashara Hora Shastra Ch.24; Phaladeepika
